title: St. Patrick's Day

jokes.text: Nature's viagra. Two of my favorite joke areas combined. Gonna be a good day.
jokes.setup: Michael casually mentions nature's viagra and his favorite joke areas
jokes.punchline: The inappropriate oversharing combined with his enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael inappropriate oversharing that's perfectly in character. Sets the tone for his delusional optimism.

jokes.text: It is the closest that the irish will ever get to christmas.
jokes.setup: St. Patrick's Day is a huge deal in Scranton
jokes.punchline: The absurd comparison to Christmas for Irish people
jokes.explanation: Sharp, quotable line with perfect economy. Absurd logic delivered with Michael's confidence makes it work.

jokes.text: Mega-desk. Command central. Surveillance, scanning, and business.
jokes.setup: Jim asks what Dwight has created
jokes.punchline: Dwight's over-the-top naming and description of his desk setup
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight absurdist logic with his grandiose naming conventions. 'Mega-desk' is inherently funny.

jokes.text: Tweedledee and tweedledum ass have been away on maternity leave. But now tweedledum ass is back, and we have a problem.
jokes.setup: Dwight refers to Jim and Pam returning from maternity leave
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous 'tweedledum ass' naming convention
jokes.explanation: Dwight's weird insult naming is character-appropriate but not particularly clever structurally.

jokes.text: Getting more...Mega-desk. That is all I care about.
jokes.setup: Dwight admits he got hooked on mega-desk
jokes.punchline: His obsessive single-mindedness about the desk
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Dwight's desk obsession with the pause and emphasis on 'more...Mega-desk'.

jokes.text: My dogs love peein' in that snow. Makes me think they're on to somethin'.
jokes.setup: Jo is talking about missing Scranton
jokes.punchline: The implication that she might want to pee in snow like her dogs
jokes.explanation: Unexpected and slightly crude observation delivered matter-of-factly in Jo's style.

jokes.text: Have I been that naughty? / No, no, no. That is a good gift, actually. Uh, buildings here in scranton are literally powered by coal.
jokes.setup: Jo receives a lump of coal as a gift and asks if she's been naughty
jokes.punchline: Michael's earnest explanation that coal is actually useful in Scranton
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff with Michael's characteristic attempt to make something awkward seem reasonable.

jokes.text: We don't get much coal down in tallahassee. I mean, just alligators and some of the worst chinese food you've ever tasted.
jokes.setup: Continuing the coal conversation
jokes.punchline: The random combination of alligators and bad Chinese food
jokes.explanation: Mild regional humor with Jo's casual delivery, but not particularly sharp.

jokes.text: When you work for sabre, only one thing matters. And I don't care if you're a loser or you practice bestiality, if jo likes you, you are in.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining how Sabre works
jokes.punchline: The escalation to bestiality as an extreme example
jokes.explanation: Michael's inappropriate escalation is in character but relies on shock value rather than cleverness.

jokes.text: It's actually my sister's old field hockey skirt.
jokes.setup: Someone compliments Andy's kilt
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's wearing his sister's field hockey skirt
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy moment - trying to be impressive but revealing something embarrassing with complete sincerity.

jokes.text: Because according to how I met your mother, that's the date that your kids are gonna wait patiently to hear about, and you'd better have a good story for them.
jokes.setup: Andy explaining why his first date with Erin has to be perfect
jokes.punchline: Using the TV show as a life guide with complete seriousness
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Andy's pop culture obsession and romantic idealism.

jokes.text: False. I am an island. And this island is volcanic. And it is about to erupt with the molten hot lava of strategy.
jokes.setup: Dwight responding to 'no man is an island'
jokes.punchline: The increasingly ridiculous volcanic strategy metaphor
jokes.explanation: Peak Dwight absurdist logic with perfect escalation. The volcanic strategy metaphor is brilliantly ridiculous and quotable.

jokes.text: The baby thinks that the refrigerator is its father.
jokes.setup: Michael talking about Jim being away from his baby
jokes.punchline: The absurd claim about the baby's paternal confusion
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Michael logic delivered with complete confidence. Great economy and visual.

jokes.text: I thought my mother was my father and my wet nurse was my mother. / That's a common mistake.
jokes.setup: Dwight shares his confusing childhood
jokes.punchline: Jim's deadpan 'that's a common mistake'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim straight-man response to Dwight's bizarre oversharing. The deadpan delivery sells it.

jokes.text: Turned out fine for me, but mose... Oh...Same story, different ending.
jokes.setup: Dwight saying his childhood confusion turned out fine
jokes.punchline: The ominous implication about Mose's outcome
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with great economy. The pause and delivery imply something terrible happened to Mose.

jokes.text: In your nose. / Yes.
jokes.setup: Andy asks if Erin is sick, she says she has indigestion
jokes.punchline: Andy pointing out it's 'in your nose' and Erin's matter-of-fact agreement
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective setup/punchline showing Erin's innocent confusion about medical terms.

jokes.text: Except once when I was in the hospital from age three to six.
jokes.setup: Erin saying whenever she's sick it goes away within hours
jokes.punchline: The casual mention of a three-year hospitalization
jokes.explanation: Classic Erin dark childhood reveal delivered with complete innocence. The contrast is perfect.

jokes.text: Oh, jo, I have an idea for suntan lotion soap.
jokes.setup: Jo asking for employee ideas after mentioning liquid paper
jokes.punchline: Kevin's nonsensical product idea
jokes.explanation: Kevin's typical non-sequitur idea that makes no sense but fits his character perfectly.

jokes.text: Oscar, homosexual accountant.
jokes.setup: Jo addressing Oscar
jokes.punchline: Her reductive, inappropriate introduction
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable corporate insensitivity delivered matter-of-factly. Shows Jo's problematic management style.

jokes.text: Darryl. Mellow, soulful, smart for warehouse.
jokes.setup: Jo continuing her reductive employee descriptions
jokes.punchline: The patronizing 'smart for warehouse' qualifier
jokes.explanation: Builds on the pattern while adding racial undertones. The 'for warehouse' qualifier is particularly cringe.

jokes.text: Look at that picture that you drew. Nice job. We're very proud of you.
jokes.setup: Darryl presents a professional shipping diagram
jokes.punchline: Jo treating it like a child's drawing
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe moment showing Jo's condescending attitude. The contrast between professional work and childish praise is perfect.

jokes.text: You know what, we are going to tape that up on the refrigerator in the kitchen.
jokes.setup: Following up on treating Darryl's work like a child's drawing
jokes.punchline: The literal suggestion to put it on the refrigerator
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the condescending treatment. Takes the metaphor to its logical extreme.

jokes.text: How about July 4th weekend? / Oh, honey, you didn't buy a ticket. / I did.
jokes.setup: Michael suggesting a visit to Jo's house
jokes.punchline: His admission that he already bought tickets
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael presumptuous behavior creating maximum awkwardness. The timing and delivery work well.

jokes.text: In the spirit of full disclosure, I have actually reserved a bunch of different seats on a bunch of different flights.
jokes.setup: Michael already admitting to buying tickets
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's booked multiple flights
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Michael's desperation and poor planning. Makes the situation even more uncomfortable.

jokes.text: Enough!
jokes.setup: Michael's continued desperate attempts to visit
jokes.punchline: Jo's sharp cutoff
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective punchline to the escalating awkwardness. One word that cuts through Michael's rambling.

jokes.text: Jo doesn't like michael anymore... Mm.
jokes.setup: Jim explaining the chain of events to camera
jokes.punchline: The understated 'Mm' reaction to the situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim reaction - understated acknowledgment of the obvious disaster. The pause and 'Mm' are perfectly timed.

jokes.text: How many knocks does it take to get you to do some work?
jokes.setup: Dwight suggesting a knocking system for communication
jokes.punchline: Jim's tootsie pop commercial reference about work
jokes.explanation: Clever reference that works as both a pop culture callback and a dig at Dwight's productivity. Good structure.

jokes.text: * Cats in the cradle and the silver spoon * [Jim trying to work while Dwight sings loudly]
jokes.setup: Dwight asks to play music at low volume
jokes.punchline: Him singing loudly instead, disrupting Jim's call
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup with Dwight's literal interpretation and Jim's increasing frustration during the phone call.

jokes.text: I'm not surprised that dwight's using my baby to steal my desk. But I'm a little surprised that it's working.
jokes.setup: Jim reflecting on the desk situation
jokes.punchline: His admission that Dwight's baby manipulation is actually effective
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim self-awareness. The structure of expecting one thing but being surprised by another is well-crafted.

jokes.text: She's still gonna like me in a week. Right?
jokes.setup: Andy rationalizing that postponing his date is fine
jokes.punchline: His sudden insecurity seeking validation from the camera
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Andy's anxiety beneath his confident exterior. The direct camera address works well.

jokes.text: A real hoop dreams story you got there.
jokes.setup: Commenting on Darryl's promotion
jokes.punchline: The inappropriate sports movie reference to a workplace advancement
jokes.explanation: Michael's tone-deaf reference with racial undertones. Character-appropriate but relies on awkwardness rather than cleverness.

jokes.text: Does her family owe your family something in terms of a past injustice?
jokes.setup: Michael can't believe Darryl got promoted on merit
jokes.punchline: His suggestion that it must be reparations-related
jokes.explanation: Peak Michael inappropriate racial commentary. Cringeworthy but fits his character's blind spots perfectly.

jokes.text: Now mike, I have to ask you to leave so that I can learn about this tiny television.
jokes.setup: Michael lingering in Darryl's new office
jokes.punchline: Darryl's polite but firm dismissal to examine his computer
jokes.explanation: Perfect Darryl response with the charming 'tiny television' description of a computer monitor. Great economy and character voice.

jokes.text: Well, if you can put your name on this day and be proud of the amount of work you've done, then by all means, you should toodle on home.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to leave at reasonable time
jokes.punchline: Jo's passive-aggressive guilt trip with 'toodle on home'
jokes.explanation: Excellent passive-aggressive boss behavior. The condescending 'toodle on home' is perfectly delivered.

jokes.text: Just because jo has no life does not mean that the rest of us don't have lives.
jokes.setup: Michael frustrated with Jo's work demands
jokes.punchline: His blunt assessment of Jo's personal life
jokes.explanation: Good Michael rant with accurate observation about workaholic bosses. Shows his frustration clearly.

jokes.text: Hey, pac-man. / Hey, what's up, butt plug?
jokes.setup: Michael calling Todd Packer
jokes.punchline: Packer's crude greeting response
jokes.explanation: Typical Packer crude humor. Character-appropriate but not particularly clever or original.

jokes.text: Hike up your skirt and pull out your tampon, borrow some balls and come meet me!
jokes.setup: Michael explaining he's stuck at work
jokes.punchline: Packer's extremely inappropriate motivational speech
jokes.explanation: Peak Packer offensiveness. Character-appropriate but relies on shock value rather than wit. Crude without being clever.

jokes.text: I'm in my jammy jams. / That's okay, I'm in my worky works.
jokes.setup: Erin answering door in pajamas
jokes.punchline: Andy's cute parallel construction response
jokes.explanation: Perfectly sweet character moment with great wordplay. The parallel construction is clever and fits both characters perfectly.

jokes.text: Or marryin' rich. And I did both.
jokes.setup: Jo talking about becoming powerful through hard work
jokes.punchline: Her matter-of-fact admission about also marrying rich
jokes.explanation: Excellent Jo moment showing her pragmatic worldview. The casual admission undercuts her inspiring speech perfectly.

jokes.text: So the baby can experience a strong male presence. [pause] No? Nothing? Okay.
jokes.setup: Michael offering to visit Jim's house
jokes.punchline: His awkward explanation followed by obvious rejection
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael inappropriate offer with perfect awkward silence. His 'No? Nothing?' acknowledgment of rejection is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: And by the way, seriously, we never, never do this.
jokes.setup: Jim explaining his evening meeting to Jo
jokes.punchline: His over-emphasis on how unusual late meetings are
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Jim's desperation to establish this isn't normal. The repetition of 'never, never' works well.

jokes.text: Your shirt tucked in? / Oh, yeah. Um. I must have did that when I used the bathroom.
jokes.setup: Michael's friends noticing his tucked-in shirt
jokes.punchline: His embarrassed excuse about tucking it in the bathroom
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy about Michael's corporate appearance clashing with his desire to seem cool. The grammatical error adds authenticity.

jokes.text: Well, any brother of erin's is a friend of mine. / Cold hands.
jokes.setup: Andy being polite to Erin's foster brother
jokes.punchline: Reed's blunt, unfriendly response about Andy's cold hands
jokes.explanation: Perfect awkward dating situation with the unwelcoming foster brother. The abrupt 'Cold hands' response is perfectly uncomfortable.

jokes.text: Plus, you don't have to sit next to this big dork and her smelly feet. / Hey, my feet aren't smelly. / They smell like roses. Smell them.
jokes.setup: Reed insulting Erin to Andy
jokes.punchline: Erin's innocent defense leading to offering her feet for smelling
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from sibling teasing to Erin's innocent over-compensation. The foot-smelling offer is perfectly awkward.

jokes.text: Nice skirt. / It's a kilt.
jokes.setup: Reed commenting on Andy's outfit
jokes.punchline: Andy's defensive correction
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective callback to earlier reveal. Reed's casual emasculation of Andy works well.

jokes.text: It's not to celebrate st. Patrick's day. It's so I can protest st. Patrick's day.
jokes.setup: Oscar explaining his eagerness to leave work
jokes.punchline: His contrarian reason for wanting to leave
jokes.explanation: Perfect Oscar moment showing his contrarian intellectualism. The logic is perfectly in character and cleverly structured.

jokes.text: I could get a girlfriend, wouldn't have to go to amsterdam seven times a year.
jokes.setup: Gabe talking about dating when he's dead
jokes.punchline: The implication about his Amsterdam trips
jokes.explanation: Dark Gabe humor with subtle prostitution implication. Character-appropriate social awkwardness delivered matter-of-factly.

jokes.text: I want to have been married by the time I would've turned 30.
jokes.setup: Michael reflecting on Gabe's sad single life
jokes.punchline: His awkward grammar about future marriage goals
jokes.explanation: Michael's convoluted grammar reveals his anxiety about marriage. The awkward tense construction is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Very, very solid all the way arod.
jokes.setup: Michael praising his employees to Jo
jokes.punchline: Using 'arod' instead of 'around'
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael malapropism mixing baseball player with 'around'. Perfect character moment showing his nervous energy.

jokes.text: As the irish poet bobby mcferrin said, 'don't worry, be happy.' Whoops.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to sound wise about the evening
jokes.punchline: Calling Bobby McFerrin an Irish poet and immediately realizing the mistake
jokes.explanation: Excellent Michael moment combining cultural ignorance with immediate self-correction. The 'Whoops' acknowledgment makes it perfect.

tmdb_overview: Michael impresses Jo; Dwight causes trouble; Andy and Erin date.
